ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: Nfono mɔ dɔɔbo mɔ adega mɔ mɛ baa taasɛ mɔ yɛɛ, ‘Ɔbelɛnsɛ, iŋ gyɛ awayu ne fo duu fo-ndɔɔ mɔ dɔ? Fonɛ dɔ ne ifɛ idɛ ɔnan i lii?’
translated: If you are looking for something to eat, and you see a beautiful, ripe fruit, would you not say, 'This is good, it is sweet and delicious?'  Is it not good to eat something that is beautiful and delicious?

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Gɛbunono gɛbono gɛ dɛ kpe Wurubuaarɛ asɛ gɛkyena mɔ dɔ mɔ gɛŋ ŋmangɛ, ne ɔkpa ɔbono ɔ dɛ kpe gɛnen gɛbunono mɔ asɛ mɔ gyɛ daa gɛkpasuɛɛ. Asa kalɛsɛ ne n kya wu gɛnen ɔkpa ɔbono bii. Asa Ako Mɛ Kya Kɛɛ Abon Yɛɛ Wurubuaarɛ Ɔkalan Ne Mɛ Kya Tɔgɛ
translated: The one who is wise, the one who is wise, knows that the world is full of good and bad, and that the one who is wise knows how to choose the good and avoid the bad. text: Mɛ lɛɛ gɛnɔ yɛɛ, “Aye asɛ gɛrɛ a bo daa ibodobodo inun de iken inyɔ.”
translated: If you want to see the sun rise, you must wake up early.

ref: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
text: Imɔso mɛ laarɛ de mɛꞌ keda mɔ. Mɛ kii mɛ selɛ sakpii ɔbono ɔ bo nno mɔ gifuu. I lii fɛɛ sakpii mɔ gi sɔɔ Yesu gyi yɛɛ ɔ gyɛ Wurubuaarɛ ikalan ɔtɔgɛbo.
translated: I have come to bring good news to you. I have come to tell you that the kingdom of God is near. Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: Don't stop him! No one who works miracles in my name will soon turn and say something bad about me.
text: Ne Yesu gi lɛɛ gɛnɔ sa mɔ yɛɛ, “Fɛ san wu gɛnen mɔ, fɛŋꞌ baa gya mɔ. Ɔbono ɔ dɛ me-ginyen ɔ kya wɔra amunale ilaa mɔ, gɛnen kaasɛ ɔbono maŋ baa kii tɔgɛ ilaa nyɛnyɛn iko kyu lii me so ayaa abono so.
translated: But Jesus said to them, “Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me. For whoever wants to save their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life for me will find it. What good will it be for someone to gain the whole world, yet forfeit their soul? Or what can anyone give in exchange for their soul?

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Wura Hɛrodɛ lɛwu gɛmara mɔ, Wurubuaarɛ dɔ ɔsɔɔ ɔko gi baa lɛɛ mɔ-nyoro nyiile Gyosɛfo oderi dɔ Igyipiti.
translated: When Herod heard that the wise men had gone to Bethlehem, he was disturbed, and he secretly sent for the wise men and asked them where the Christ child was born.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Gɛkɛ gɛdɛ mɔ, nyangbon de afuu belɛ a baa da, ne ibon i bɔla baa da de obu mɔ. Obu mɔ yii daa giŋɛsii so so mɔ, ɔ tɔrɔ daa wurum!”
translated: If you are going to do something, do it well. If you are going to do something, do it well and do not leave anything undone. Do not leave anything undone, do it thoroughly!

ref: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
text: Gyɔn akasɛbo mɔ mɛ nu gɛnen mɔ, mɛ kpaa kyu mɔ-gibuni mɔ kyu kpaa pule, ne mɛ kpaa buu sa Yesu. Yesu Gi Sa Sakpii Belɛ Agyudɔ Ne Mɛ Gyi Kaabo
translated: When the evil one comes to you, do not be afraid, because you are not alone. You have the power of God within you. You can overcome him. You can overcome him through the name of Jesus. Jesus is the savior of the world and you are his child.

ref: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
text: ne mɛ lii nkyan mɔ dɔ. Yesu gi kyingi mɔ, imɔ gɛmara mɔ, gɛnen ibuni ibono mɛ naa baa loo Gyɛrusalem ɔsowolɛ ɔbono Wurubuaarɛ gi lɔrɔ yela sa mɔ-ɔson mɔ, ne mɛ lɛɛ mɔmɔ-nyoro gɛwi nyiile asa sakyɔ.
translated: When Jesus was in Jerusalem during the Feast of Tabernacles, he went up to the temple and taught. The Jews were amazed at his teachings, because he spoke with authority unlike anyone they had ever heard.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Aberɛ abono mɔ, akuuri gikpen belɛ giko gi yelɛ gibii mɔ alan dɔ nno mɛ kya gyi.
translated: If you are not careful, you will fall into trouble.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: “Ɔbelɛnsɛ, Wurubuaarɛ nbara mɔ dɔ mɔ, nmɔ mɔ ne n tansi gi tiri daa?”
translated: Why are you looking at me with that kind of anger, are you angry with me?

ref: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text: Yesu gi tɔgɛ sa ɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Fɛꞌ kerɛ fɛye-nyoro so dɔɔdan. Farasii awura de Saadusii awura ilaa i dɛ fɛɛ gɛlin gɛbono gɛ kya yɛgɛ bodobodo kya ŋan mɔ.”
translated: Jesus said to them, "Truly I tell you, it will be hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven. Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God."

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Me so mɔ, mɛ laa biidɛ fɛye ako kpe abɛyin abelɛnsɛ de awura-awura ansi dɔ. Mɛ kyu fɛye kpe gɛnen mɔ, i laa bugi ɔkpa sa fɛye de fɛꞌ nyɛ tɔgɛ me-ilaa sa mɔmɔ‑rɛ gɛsinkpan so asa kpɛi-kpɛi.
translated: I am so happy, I will always love you with all my heart. I will always love you, even if you are far away, because your love is my joy and my strength.

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Obolonbu mɔ gi lii kpaa taasɛ mɔ-nyi yɛɛ, “Menɛ ne nꞌ tɔgɛ wura mɔ yɛɛ ɔꞌ sa me?” Ne mɔ-nyi yɛɛ, “Tɔgɛ mɔ yɛɛ Gyɔn ɔbono ɔ gyere asa Wurubuaarɛ sagyere mɔ gimu ne fo kya laarɛ.”
translated: Obolonbu asked the man, "What did you do to my son?" The man replied, "I gave your son Gyɔn, the child of Wurubuaarɛ, a medicine to cure his illness."

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: de iꞌ nyɛ kyena de ilaa ibono i bo Wurubuaarɛ ɔwolɛ mɔ agyɛbi dɔ mɔ. I bo nno yɛɛ, ‘Mɛ laa kpɛ mɛ kya kerɛ ilaa, mɛ maŋ kii wu sɛi; Mɛ laa kpɛ mɛ kya nu ilaa, mɛ maŋ kii nu ilaa gɛsɛ. Nkana mɛ nyɛ nu ilaa gɛsɛ mɔ, mɛ laa naa kii ba Wurubuaarɛ asɛ de Wurubuaarɛ oꞌ kyu mɔmɔ-ilaa nyɛnyɛn kyɛɛ mɔmɔ.’ ”
translated: “Then he said, ‘I will make a covenant with you and your descendants after you, and I will give you the land of Canaan, as an everlasting possession. I will be their God, and they will be my people. And I will be with them, and I will be their God. And they will know that I am the Lord their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt, that I might dwell among them.’”
